name: Tavastia Proper (Finland)

colors.symbolism: the gules field of the historic Tavastian arms
the golden charges of the arms

description: A rectangular red flag charged centrally with the gold and silver provincial arms of Tavastia Proper.
history: Tavastia Proper (Kanta-Häme) is one of the regions formed in Finland's 1997 administrative reform. Its banner derives from the historic arms of the medieval Häme province.

features.symbolism: a heraldic composition derived from the historic provincial arms of Tavastia (Häme), incorporating a crowned lynx and trefoil-like flower motifs in gold on a red field, evoking the medieval Häme province in southern inland Finland.
